course_info.title: Physics
course_info.description: ZIMSEC Advanced Level Physics for Form 5 develops a deep understanding of the fundamental principles that govern the physical universe. This syllabus focuses on building strong analytical and problem-solving skills through topics such as General Physics, Newtonian Mechanics, Oscillations and Waves, and Electricity and Magnetism, preparing learners for further scientific studies.

topics.title: Physical Quantities and Units
topics.description: Learners define and use base and derived units, check equation homogeneity, understand prefixes, and represent vectors with perpendicular components.

topics.title: Errors and Uncertainties
topics.description: Learners distinguish between systematic and random errors, differentiate precision and accuracy, and assess uncertainty in derived quantities.

topics.title: Kinematics
topics.description: Learners derive and apply equations for uniformly accelerated linear motion, analyze projectile motion, and identify everyday examples of rectilinear and non-linear motion.

topics.title: Dynamics
topics.description: Learners define linear momentum, apply conservation of momentum to collisions, understand impulse, and describe applications of Newton's laws.

topics.title: Forces
topics.description: Learners describe forces on objects, calculate upthrust using Archimedes' Principle, explain friction and terminal velocity, and define moments and torque.

topics.title: Work, Energy and Power
topics.description: Learners define work, kinetic energy, and potential energy, differentiate energy types, explain internal energy and efficiency, and relate power to energy conversion.

topics.title: Circular Motion
topics.description: Learners express angular displacement, define angular velocity and centripetal force, and apply equations to describe circular motion examples.

topics.title: Gravitational Field
topics.description: Learners understand gravitational fields, define field strength, apply Newton's law of gravitation, and analyze circular orbits and gravitational potential.

topics.title: Oscillations
topics.description: Learners describe simple harmonic motion, define oscillatory terms, graphically represent displacement, velocity, and acceleration, and explain damped and forced oscillations including resonance.

topics.title: Waves
topics.description: Learners define critical angle and total internal reflection, apply wave terminology, understand polarization, describe the electromagnetic spectrum, and explain X-ray production and uses.

topics.title: Superposition
topics.description: Learners explain the principle of superposition, distinguish stationary and progressive waves, identify nodes and antinodes, and understand two-source interference and diffraction.

topics.title: Electricity
topics.description: Learners describe electrostatic phenomena, define charge, potential difference, resistance, and e.m.f., and solve problems involving electrical circuits and internal resistance.

topics.title: D.C. Circuits
topics.description: Learners apply Kirchhoff's laws to solve circuit problems, use potential dividers, and explain the principle of the potentiometer.

topics.title: Electric Fields
topics.description: Learners describe electric fields and field strength, apply Coulomb's law, analyze charged particle motion, and define electric potential and potential gradient.

topics.title: Capacitances
topics.description: Learners describe capacitor function, define capacitance, solve problems with series and parallel capacitors, deduce energy stored, and describe charging and discharging of capacitors in RC circuits.
